fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arizona Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arizona - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity arizona, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to arizona, respiratory therapist compact arizona, arizona respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Arizona Board of Respiratory Care Examiners
board.parent_dept: Arizona does not mandate state dietitian licensure. The national CDR credential is the recognized standard.

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Arizona.

steps.title: Complete an Accredited Respiratory Therapy Program
steps.description: Graduate from a respiratory therapy education program accredited by the Commission on Accreditation for Respiratory Care (CoARC) or its successor.

steps.title: Pass the NBRC Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) Therapist Multiple Choice (TMC) Examination and Clinical Simulation Examination (CSE).

steps.title: Apply for a License
steps.description: Submit a completed application, including required documentation and fees, to the Arizona Board of Respiratory Care Examiners.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Arizona respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Arizona have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Arizona. To work in Arizona, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Arizona?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Arizona
hero.dek: Arizona Respiratory Therapist licenses require graduation from an accredited program, passing the NBRC examination, and submitting an application to the Arizona Board of Respiratory Care Examiners.
